page: Amazon Web Services m5d.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, offering 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

A key feature of this instance is its bundled local storage, consisting of a 300 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. This local storage provides low-latency, high-speed temporary storage directly attached to the host. For connectivity, the instance offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this configuration.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU capabilities and memory bandwidth, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores falling into the middle 50% tier. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by lower-tier results in single-threaded stress-ng, Geekbench, and compression tasks. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput also perform at average levels, while database operations and LLM inference tasks generally register in the lower performance brackets.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5d.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring local NVMe storage without the premium of dedicated storage-optimized instances. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and applications that benefit from temporary high-speed local scratch space, rather than compute-intensive or heavy database operations.

It delivers 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores) running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of a 300 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, though single-core execution and database operations fall into lower percentiles. This instance provides a balanced resource profile, making it qualitatively cost-effective for workloads that require temporary local storage. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, and static web serving.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for temporary workloads.
